design_philosophy.core_loop: Infiltrate enemy base while avoiding detection; obtain key cards and equipment to unlock new areas; rescue hostages to increase rank and carrying capacity; engage boss mercenaries in combat encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Stealth vs. discovery - maintaining infiltration while managing Alert Mode escalation from single-enemy detection to full reinforcement waves; resource scarcity (limited ammunition, health recovery items)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone operative conducting solo espionage mission; tactical infiltration with improvisation and adaptation to discovered threats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering stealth-first design philosophy where avoidance is preferred over confrontation; information gathering via radio transceiver creates asymmetrical knowledge gameplay; rank system ties player progression to non-lethal rescue objectives rather than combat performance; heavily contextual enemy behavior tied to visual detection and noise generation

difficulty.lives_system: Continues system with password-based progression checkpoints
difficulty.continues_system: 6 fixed respawn positions determined by rank level or event triggers (capture, supercomputer destruction); allows tactical death-warping for speedrun optimization
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based save system (MSX2 required tape drive or Game Master II cartridge; later versions eliminated these complications)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through rank management system - player can intentionally reduce rank to access faster respawn positions; ability to choose stealth vs. confrontation approach; optional hostage rescues create difficulty scaling through rank progression

progression.upgrade_system: Rank system (0-4 stars) unlocked by rescuing hostages in groups of 5; higher ranks increase carrying capacity and maximum health; demotion occurs if rescued hostages are killed. Equipment progression through key card acquisition and item discovery.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Multi-stage key card system to unlock doors; hostage rescue gates rank-based access; equipment unlocks tied to exploration and boss defeats (rocket launcher requires rank 4)
replayability_hooks: Password system enables multiple entry points and rank configurations
Flexible hostage rescue ordering (16 available, only 15 required for rank 4)
Optional boss encounters based on equipment availability
Death-warp optimization creates speedrun vs. standard play divergence
Three building complex with multiple floor levels encourages non-linear exploration within semi-linear structure

world_building_techniques: Radio transceiver dialogue system with four frequency-locked contacts (Big Boss, Schneider, Diane, Jennifer) providing contextual area-specific information
Hostage rescue narrative - named prisoners scattered throughout buildings create rescue objectives with character-driven motivation
Metal Gear weapon exposition through radio calls explaining the threat's capabilities
Equipment discovery tied to story beats (transmitter attachment warnings, stealth suit acquisition)
NES version contains unused MSX2 dialogue entries (Big Boss heat panel warning, Schneider elevator guidance) showing narrative compression in localization

boss_design.philosophy: Mercenary encounters serve as contextual obstacle encounters rather than purely mechanical challenges; boss selection tied to equipment choices and infiltration pathway
notable_bosses: Grey Fox - rescue objective and boss encounter integrated into narrative
Fire Trooper - basement guard in Building 2 variant (NES restructuring)
Twin Shot - rooftop mercenary with hostage co-location creating risk/reward tension
Coward Duck - mentioned as tactical waypoint for death-warp optimization

faction_indicators: FOXHOUND unit affiliation (player character Solid Snake)
Underground resistance network (Schneider, Diane, Jennifer contacts)
danger_signals: Alert Mode escalation from single-enemy exclamation mark to double-exclamation reinforcement waves
Infrared sensors and security cameras trigger high-alert reinforcement spawning
Unsuppressed weapon fire creates audio-based detection and pursuit
Building progression signals increasing threat density (basement levels, rooftops)
environmental_storytelling: Electrified floors requiring specific equipment to traverse safely
Hollow walls requiring explosives to breach (obstacle-based area gating)
Hostage cells scattered throughout buildings establishing prisoner narrative
Multi-building complex architecture mirrors military base organization
Equipment placement (suppressors, gas masks, binoculars) contextualizes tactical infiltration requirements
